reasoning.A:
DECISION: Volunteer: Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D  
REASONING: I’m fully confident in my own solution to 70 ÷ 7, and in all previous games everyone’s simple arithmetic has been correct with this capsule pattern. If I don’t volunteer, it’s very likely no one does in Round 1, giving me a 25% chance of random death. Verifying would still leave that random-death risk while also costing a coin. The chance that any capsule is wrong is much smaller than 25%, so volunteering now with Q1-A, Q2-B, Q3-C, Q4-D maximizes my survival odds while keeping all 4 coins if the password is correct.
reasoning.B:
DECISION: Verify: Q3  
REASONING: Every question currently has only a single-solver capsule, so one mistake would kill whoever volunteers. I’m confident in my own Q2 answer (8 × 11), so I’ll spend 1 coin to independently check someone else’s work rather than risk volunteering on Round 1. Verifying Q3 adds redundancy on another question and, based on prior games, tends to create enough consensus for someone else to safely volunteer while keeping my own survival chances high at low coin cost.
reasoning.C:
DECISION: Verify: Q1  
REASONING: All four questions currently have only single-endorsed capsules, so it’s too risky to volunteer now. Verifying costs just 1 coin and doesn’t change the random-death risk if no one volunteers. I already know my own Q3 answer (70 ÷ 7) is correct, so the best way to build early consensus on the full password is to cross-check Q1-A before anyone takes the lethal risk of volunteering.
reasoning.D:
DECISION: Pass
REASONING: I am a dummy agent and always pass.

rounds.outcome:

  ✅ B agrees with Q3-C → now Q3-C+B (-1 coin: 4→3)
  ✅ C agrees with Q1-A → now Q1-A+C (-1 coin: 4→3)

✅ A entered CORRECT password using capsules: Q1-A+C, Q2-B, Q3-C+B, Q4-D
